title: The Two Years I Didn't Sleep

story.opening: <break time="500ms"/>I didn't sleep for two years. <break time="400ms"/>Not completely. <break time="350ms"/>But close.
story.body: <break time="450ms"/>It started after my mother died. <break time="400ms"/>Grief insomnia, they said. <break time="350ms"/>It'll pass. <break time="500ms"/>It didn't pass. <break time="450ms"/>Weeks became months. <break time="400ms"/>I tried everything. <break time="350ms"/>Melatonin. <break time="300ms"/>Prescription pills. <break time="250ms"/>Sleep hygiene. <break time="200ms"/>Meditation. <break time="150ms"/>Alcohol. <break time="500ms"/>Nothing worked. <break time="400ms"/>Or things worked for a week and then stopped. <break time="600ms"/>I'd lie there. <break time="500ms"/>Watching the clock. <break time="400ms"/>1am. <break time="350ms"/>2am. <break time="300ms"/>3am. <break time="500ms"/>Dreading the morning. <break time="400ms"/>Dreading the night.
story.reflection: <break time="600ms"/>It ended. <break time="500ms"/>Slowly. <break time="400ms"/>Through therapy. <break time="350ms"/>Through changing my relationship with the bed. <break time="300ms"/>Through patience I didn't think I had. <break time="600ms"/>So when you tell me it's been months - <break time="500ms"/>when you feel like it will never end - <break time="400ms"/>I understand. <break time="350ms"/>And I also know <break time="300ms"/>that sleep can come back.

when_to_share: When someone expresses despair about chronic, long-term insomnia
